summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing for StraitsSpace (Rep Dashboard, Customer Chat, Admin). 21/21 pytest cases passed (100% backend) including DeepSeek-backed /api/qualify, simulators, leads CRUD/reply, products CRUD, chat with hydrated product cards, wallet auto-create + topup + deduct, order with insufficient balance (400), admin analytics + chat logs, and Telegram webhook 403. All three UI flows verified via Playwright: Rep Dashboard (metrics toggle, simulator injects + re-ranks, conversation drawer + editable draft + send-reply advancing status), Customer Chat (Pak Budi reply + 3 product cards on 'running shoes under $100', checkout dialog → place order → confirmation), Admin (add product visible, orders + status selects render, chatbot logs + escalations render).

ui_bugs.issue: send-reply-btn renders below initial viewport at 1080p; user must scroll inside drawer to reach Send. Functional but not ideal — consider sticky footer for the draft editor + Send button.

issues: Radix Dialog accessibility warnings in console: DialogContent missing DialogTitle (screen-reader a11y) and missing Description/aria-describedby. Affects CustomerChat checkout-dialog, topup dialog, RepDashboard conversation drawer, Admin add-product dialog.

action_items: Optional: Add VisuallyHidden DialogTitle + DialogDescription to all Sheet/Dialog components (Customer checkout, Topup, Conversation drawer, Admin product form) to silence Radix a11y warnings.
Optional: Make the Pak Budi draft editor + Send button sticky-positioned at the bottom of the conversation drawer so users don't have to scroll inside the drawer.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 766 lines — borderline; consider splitting into routers (leads, products, chat, wallet, admin, telegram) once features stabilize.
compute_priority is correctly computed server-side per the PRD formula and is the single source of truth (LLM never sets priority) — good.
All Mongo reads use projection {_id:0} consistently — no ObjectId leakage observed in responses.
DeepSeek call has graceful fallback to heuristic_qualify on exception — good resilience.
All interactive elements expose data-testid as specified — testability is excellent.
Telegram webhook correctly rejects wrong secret with 403; the try/except in handler always returns {ok:true} on internal errors which is correct Telegram behavior.
POST /api/orders accepts arbitrary item.price from client — in production this should be re-priced server-side from product catalog to prevent price tampering. Acceptable for v0 demo.
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers ignore credentials with wildcard, fine for demo but should be tightened in prod.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in v0 per user choice
seed_data_creation: Backend seeds 8 products + 7 leads on first startup via seed(); confirmed present. Tests create TEST_-prefixed products/leads (not cleaned up but isolated by prefix).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL: https://lead-priority-hub-2.preview.emergentagent.com . Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v` for regression. DeepSeek key is live in backend/.env so /api/qualify and /api/chat hit real LLM (slow ~3-6s). Frontend polls leads + metrics every 4s. All data-testids in the review_request match the implementation. The Telegram webhook is reachable but cannot be exercised end-to-end without a real Telegram update; the 403-on-wrong-secret path is covered.
rca_of_issues: No critical issues. Only console-level a11y warnings from Radix Dialog (cosmetic) and a layout nuance where the Send button in the conversation drawer sits below the fold at 1080p — both non-blocking.
